02 January 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • Russia asks US to stop chasing Venezuela-linked oil tanker Press TV 02 Jan 2026 -- Russia has formally asked the United States stop chasing an oil tanker that had been sailing for Venezuela to pick up oil, a report says.
  • Venezuela Ready to Discuss Anti-Drug Agreement With US - Maduro Sputnik 02 Jan 2026 -- Venezuela is ready to discuss an agreement with the United States to combat drug trafficking, but it must be a serious discussion, President Nicolas Maduro said.

  • Coast Guard suspends search for individuals in the Pacific Ocean US Coast Guard 02 Jan 2026 -- The U.S. Coast Guard suspended its search for the reported people in the water approximately 400 nautical miles southwest of the Mexico/Guatemala border Friday.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • 449th AEG Airmen Test Readiness During Exercise Pale Serpent DoD News 02 Jan 2026 -- Airmen assigned to the 449th Air Expeditionary Group, in conjunction with Army, Marine Corps and Navy partners, conducted readiness training at Camp Lemonnier, Djibouti, during Exercise Pale Serpent, Dec. 26-29, 2025.
  • US Navy rescues 3 Filipino fishers adrift in South China Sea Philippine News Agency 02 Jan 2026 -- Three Filipino fisherfolk were safely rescued by the crew of the USNS Cesar Chavez (T-AKE 14) after their vessel was found adrift in the South China Sea on Jan. 1, the United States 7th Fleet reported Thursday.

  • Norway Puts Ukraine Aid Ahead of Its Own Combat Readiness - Chief of Defense Sputnik 02 Jan 2026 -- Norway prioritizes aid to Ukraine over strengthening its own combat capability and replenishing its weapons stockpiles, Norwegian Chief of Defense Eirik Kristoffersen said.
  • Ministry of Defence grants Defence City's first resident status to the manufacturer of Vampire drones Ukraine MOD 02 Jan 2026
  • Ukraine attracted over $45 billion in security assistance in 2025 Ukraine MOD 02 Jan 2026
  • State Special Transport Service sappers cleared 45,259.48 hectares of Ukrainian territory in 2025 Ukraine MOD 02 Jan 2026
  • Scaling up the aviation personnel training system in 2025 Ukraine MOD 02 Jan 2026

  • PLA Navy commissions new Type 052D destroyer with enhanced capabilities Global Times 02 Jan 2026 -- The Chinese People's Liberation Army (PLA) Navy has commissioned a new Type 052D guided missile destroyer, which features upgraded radar, weapon and network systems, leading to enhanced regional air defense, sea attack and task force command capabilities, official media reported.

  • Belarus to Hold Joint Military Exercises With UAE in 2026 Sputnik 02 Jan 2026 -- The armed forces of Belarus and the UAE will conduct joint exercises in 2026, Maj. Gen. Valery Revenko, head of the Belarusian Defense Ministry's department of international military cooperation, said.

  • Defense Minister: Missile capability of Iran is non-negotiable IRNA 02 Jan 2026 -- Iran's minister of defense says that the missile capability of the Islamic Republic of Iran is non-negotiable.
  • No bomb, talks, assassination can destroy Iran's missile power: Defense chief Press TV 02 Jan 2026 -- Iranian Defense Minister Brigadier General Aziz Nasirzadeh says the country's missile power cannot be destroyed by bombs, negotiations, or the cowardly assassination of scientists and commanders.

Defense Industry

  • Contracts For Jan. 2, 2026

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • Security Council: New faces, old tensions as five nations take their seats UN News 02 Jan 2026 -- From deploying peacekeepers to conducting quiet - but at times heated - diplomacy, the UN Security Council sits at the heart of global decision-making on war and peace. As of January, five new countries will have a seat around the iconic horseshoe table.
  • Press Conference by Security Council President on Work Programme for January UN Security Council 02 Jan 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • 'Dangerous escalation': Palestinian group decries Israeli push for new settlements Press TV 02 Jan 2026 -- Palestinian Islamic Jihad has warned that Israel's approval of new settlement units in the occupied West Bank marks a "dangerous escalation" aimed at entrenching annexation and erasing Palestinian presence.

  • Statement attributable to the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General - on the occupied Palestinian territory UN Secretary-General 02 Jan 2026
  • Gaza aid lifelines under strain as winter worsens UN News 02 Jan 2026 -- Humanitarian partners in the Gaza Strip are struggling to reach hundreds of thousands of vulnerable people as winter storms batter damaged homes and temporary shelters.
  • Inter-Agency Standing Committee (IASC) Statement, Revoke planned international NGO ban, humanitarian leaders urge Israeli authorities UNICEF 02 Jan 2026 -- We urge the Israeli authorities to reconsider their plan to ban many of the international NGOs operating in the Occupied Palestinian Territory (OPT).
  • Israel: Türk says draft proposals on death penalty for Palestinians must be dropped OHCHR 02 Jan 2026 -- UN Human Rights Chief Volker Türk today urged the Israeli authorities to drop plans to introduce new legislation that would impose mandatory death sentences exclusively on Palestinians under certain circumstances, both in the Occupied Palestinian Territory and in Israel, saying the proposals fly in the face of international law on several levels.
  • Drop draft proposals on death penalty for Palestinians, UN rights chief urges Israel UN News 02 Jan 2026 -- The UN's top human rights official on Friday called on Israeli authorities to abandon plans for new legislation that would impose mandatory death sentences exclusively on Palestinians, warning the proposals violate international law and fundamental human rights standards.

  • Joint Statement by the Foreign Ministers of Pakistan, Egypt, Indonesia, Jordan, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Turkiye, and the United Arab Emirates Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 02 Jan 2026 -- The Foreign Ministers of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, the Arab Republic of Egypt, the Republic of Indonesia, the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan, the State of Qatar, the Republic of Türkiye, the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates, expressed their deepest concern regarding the deteriorating humanitarian situation in the Gaza Strip, which has been exacerbated by severe, harsh, and unstable weather conditions, including heavy rainfall and storms, and compounded by the continued lack of sufficient humanitarian access, acute shortages of essential life-saving supplies, and the slow pace of the entry of essential materials required for the rehabilitation of basic services and the establishment of temporary housing.
  • Saudi Arabia, Jordan, UAE, Indonesia, Pakistan, Türkiye, Qatar and Egypt Express Deepest Concern regarding Deteriorating Humanitarian Situation in the Gaza Strip SPA 02 Jan 2026 -- The foreign ministers of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan, the United Arab Emirates, the Republic of Indonesia, the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, the Republic of Türkiye, the State of Qatar and the Arab Republic of Egypt expressed their deepest concern regarding the deteriorating humanitarian situation in the Gaza Strip, which has been exacerbated by severe, harsh, and unstable weather conditions, including heavy rainfall and storms, and compounded by the continued lack of sufficient humanitarian access, acute shortages of essential life-saving supplies, and the slow pace of the entry of essential materials required for the rehabilitation of basic services and the establishment of temporary housing
  • Government allocates SEK 800 million in humanitarian support to Gaza and region for 2026 Government Offices of Sweden 02 Jan 2026 -- The Government has decided to allocate SEK 800 million for the humanitarian crisis in Gaza and the region in 2026, thus continuing Sweden's extensive support to the long-suffering civilian population.
  • The Spanish Government rejects the decision of the Israeli Government to apply registration regulations to humanitarian NGOs operating in Palestine Spain MFA 02 Jan 2026

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Occupied Palestinian Territory, Sudan UNOCHA 02 Jan 2026

  • Saudi airstrikes on UAE-backed separatists kill seven in southern Yemen Press TV 02 Jan 2026 -- Saudi warplanes have bombed separatists in southern Yemen backed by the United Arab Emirates, leaving seven killed, amid tensions between the two Persian Gulf Arab nations.
  • Press Remarks by the Spokesperson Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 02 Jan 2026 -- On media question seeking about the situation in Yemen, the Spokesperson of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs Mr. Tahir Andrabi stated the following:
  • UAE-allied separatists block Saudi delegation from visiting Yemen's Aden Press TV 02 Jan 2026 -- The Saudi ambassador to Yemen says the UAE-allied separatists operating in the conflict-ridden country refused landing permission for a plane carrying a delegation from Saudi Arabia to the port city of Aden.

News Reports

  • Police Have Interviewed Crew Members of the Fitburg Vessel Police of Finland 02 Jan 2026 -- The police are cooperating with various authorities in the investigation of the case. The investigative actions are ongoing, including interviews and forensic examinations.

  • FBI Disrupts Alleged New Year's Eve Attack, Man Charged with Attempting to Provide Material Support to ISIS US Dept. of Justice 02 Jan 2026 -- Today, the Justice Department and the U.S. Attorney's Office for the Western District of North Carolina announced that a Mint Hill, North Carolina man that allegedly planned to use knives and hammers to execute a deadly New Year's Eve attack at a grocery store and a fast food restaurant in support of the Islamic State in Iraq and al-Sham (ISIS) has been arrested and charged with attempting to provide material support to a foreign terrorist organization.

  • Budget deadlock continues as Legislature blocks review CNA 02 Jan 2026 -- A stalemate over the general budget for fiscal year 2026 continued Friday after the opposition-controlled Legislature again voted down a ruling Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) caucus motion to review the budget proposal, voting along party lines.
  • China military drills' disruption of air travel had a purpose: Expert CNA 02 Jan 2026 -- The disruption of 941 flights in and out of Taiwan due to China's recent large-scale military exercises was no accident but rather the result of a "quasi-blockade" used to simulate creating air and sea routes needed for an amphibious landing, a military expert said.
  • Mainland official calls for peaceful development of cross-Strait relations Global Times 02 Jan 2026 -- A Chinese mainland official on Friday called for efforts to promote peaceful development of relations across the Taiwan Strait and achieve national reunification.
  • MND spokesperson slams external forces' hype over military drills around Taiwan island; expert says State Department's cliché response fails to address root causes of tensions Global Times 02 Jan 2026 -- Responding to hypes by external forces regarding the just-concluded "Justice Mission 2025" joint drills, Chinese Defense Ministry spokesperson Zhang Xiaogang said on Friday that China will never allow any force to tie our hands in curbing "Taiwan independence" separatist activities in the name of safeguarding cross-Straits peace. He urged relevant countries to stop conniving at and supporting "Taiwan independence" forces, and stop stirring up trouble on the Taiwan question.
  • China will never allow any force to tie its hands in curbing 'Taiwan independence' separatist acts in name of safeguarding cross-Straits peace: MND spokesperson Global Times 02 Jan 2026 -- Responding to hypes by external forces regarding the just-concluded "Justice Mission 2025" joint drills, Chinese Defense Ministry spokesperson Zhang Xiaogang said on Friday that China will never allow any force to tie our hands in curbing "Taiwan independence" separatist activities in the name of safeguarding cross-Straits peace. He urged relevant countries to stop conniving at and supporting "Taiwan independence" forces, and stop stirring up trouble on the Taiwan question.
  • Press Remarks by the Spokesperson Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 02 Jan 2026 -- On media queries seeking Pakistan's response to developments related to the Taiwan Strait and the question of Taiwan, the Spokesperson of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs Mr. Tahir Andrabi stated the following:
  • Defense ministry rebuffs criticism over drills around Taiwan PRC MND 02 Jan 2026 -- The Ministry of National Defense said a recent large-scale exercise around Taiwan was intended to deter "Taiwan independence" forces and foreign interference, pushing back against criticism from several Western governments that the drills undermined regional stability.
  • Taiwan drills aimed to deter external interference: China Defense Ministry Press TV 02 Jan 2026 -- China said its latest military exercises around Taiwan are aimed at deterring foreign interference, and warning that any move crossing Beijing's red lines on the island would be met with decisive countermeasures.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 02 Jan 2026

  • U.S. urges Beijing to show 'restraint' after drills near Taiwan CNA 02 Jan 2026 -- The United States has urged Beijing to pull back on its campaign to pressure Taiwan militarily following its latest live-fire military drills around the island held earlier this week.
  • Chinese Embassy in New Zealand rejects MFAT statement on PLA exercises around Taiwan island Global Times 02 Jan 2026 -- In response to a statement posted by New Zealand's Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Trade (MFAT) on its social media account claiming "concern" over the recent military exercises around Taiwan island, a spokesperson of the Chinese Embassy in New Zealand said on Thursday that the relevant post released by New Zealand's MFAT confuses right and wrong, and China firmly opposes it. Taiwan is an inalienable part of China's territory. The Taiwan question is purely an internal affair of China and brooks no interference from any external forces, said the spokesperson.

  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Visits Kumsusan Palace of Sun KCNA 02 Jan 2026 -- All the people across the DPRK are paying the highest tribute to President Kim Il Sung and Chairman Kim Jong Il , eternal leaders of the Party, state and people and great sages of the revolution, greeting the hopeful New Year with the great pride and honor of having successfully concluded the historic struggle of 2025 for the overall development of socialist construction, rallied close around the Workers' Party of Korea.

  • President Lee pledges 'great leap forward' in New Year's speech KOREA.net 02 Jan 2026 -- President Lee Jae Myung on Jan. 1 said, "We will gallop powerfully like the Red Horse this year (2026) - the first year of a great leap forward for the Republic of Korea."

  • Trump Tells Iranian Protesters US Supports Them As Death Toll, Arrests Rise RFE/RL 02 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has voiced support for demonstrators braving brutal suppression from security forces in Iran amid protests over the country's economic woes - one of the biggest challenges to the Islamic republic's leadership in years - that have resulted in several deaths and dozens of arrests.
  • Araghchi: Iran's Armed Forces on standby IRNA 02 Jan 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has stressed that the Iranian Armed Forces are on standby and know exactly where to aim in the event of any infringement of Iranian sovereignty.
  • Araghchi says Iran's armed forces ready to respond, following Trump's 'reckless' threat Press TV 02 Jan 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has warned US President Donald Trump against meddling in Iran's internal affairs, slamming his latest remarks as "reckless and dangerous," and stressing that Iran's armed forces are fully prepared to defend the country's sovereignty.
  • Ghalibaf: Iranian people have disappointed enemies far more experienced than Trump IRNA 02 Jan 2026 -- The speaker of the Iranian Parliament says that the Iranian people have disappointed enemies far more experienced than US President Donald Trump.
  • Baqaei to Trump: Iranians will not allow foreign interference IRNA 02 Jan 2026 -- The spokesperson for the Ministry of Foreign Affairs has emphasized that in their dialogue and efforts to resolve domestic issues, Iranians will not permit any form of foreign interference.
  • Shamkhani warns Trump: Hand of meddler will be cut IRNA 02 Jan 2026 -- The Leader's representative on the Defense Council has stressed that any interventionist hand that approaches Iranian security by making excuses will be cut off before it arrives with a regrettable response.
  • Iran vows 'swift, decisive, comprehensive' response to any aggression after Trump threat Press TV 02 Jan 2026 -- Iran has warned the United States against any interference in its internal affairs, vowing a "swift, decisive, and comprehensive" response to any act of aggression following threatening remarks by US President Donald Trump.
  • Iran warns Trump against intervention as top MP says US forces 'legitimate targets' Press TV 02 Jan 2026 -- Iran on Friday warned US President Donald Trump against interfering in its internal affairs, with a Foreign Ministry spokesman rejecting any foreign meddling and a senior lawmaker saying US forces in the region would become "legitimate targets" in the event of American adventurism.
  • 'Be mindful of your soldiers,' Larijani warns US after Trump's meddlesome post Press TV 02 Jan 2026 -- Iran's top national security official has warned US President Donald Trump that any American interference in Iran's internal affairs would destroy US interests in the region.
  • Settlement on Iran's Nuclear Program Within Reach — Russian Foreign Ministry Sputnik 02 Jan 2026 -- A resolution to the standoff over Iran's nuclear program is achievable if all sides show sufficient political will, a senior Russian Foreign Ministry official told Sputnik.: the mark does not exist in the description file
  • Policy of Escalation Around Iran's Nuclear Program Erroneous - Russian Foreign Ministry Sputnik 02 Jan 2026 -- erroneous
  • Iran Braces For More Protests As Death Toll, Arrests Rise RFE/RL 02 Jan 2026 -- Iranian authorities braced for more unrest in multiple regions on January 2, with the death toll and arrests mounting after protesters angered by the country's economic woes hit the streets for the fifth day in a row.

  • Belarus Is Jailing Its Citizens For Supporting Ukraine's Fight Against Russia RFE/RL 02 Jan 2026 -- IT specialist Ihar Karatkou is in the middle of a five-year sentence at Corrective Colony No. 2, a prison in Babruysk, southeast of Minsk. Why? He donated the equivalent of about $10 to the Kastus Kalinouski Regiment, a unit of Belarusian volunteers fighting on Kyiv's side in the Russian war against Ukraine.

  • Japan-U.S. Summit Telephone Talk Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 02 Jan 2026
  • Comment by Foreign Ministry Official Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova on the revocation of the rehabilitation of Japanese war criminals Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 02 Jan 2026
